title: orbit-state v0.1 — Rust + SQLite + MCP substrate
description:
Promoted from spec: orbit/specs/2026-05-07-orbit-state-v0.1/spec.yaml

Goal: Ship orbit-state v0.1 — Rust + SQLite + MCP substrate per decision 0015, with bd verb parity for single-repo workflow, dogfooded in orbit repo itself, 4-week budget.

Cards advanced by this work: 0008 (.orbit/ folder), 0017 (setup), 0020 (orbit-state), 0021 (tasks).

Spec is the canonical source of truth — bead acceptance field is the thin checklist; full ACs with verification, halt conditions, escalations, and kill conditions live in the spec.yaml.

Reviews: review-spec-2026-05-07-v3.md (APPROVE).
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] ac-01 [gate]: Rust core canonical serde — round-trip byte-identical, atomic temp+rename writes, deny-unknown-fields strict schema conformance, multiline-body edge case fixtures (code fence, trailing blank, hard tabs, CRLF rejection)
- [x] ac-02 [gate]: SQLite index rebuildable from files alone — orbit verify mode diffs index against files
- [x] ac-03: File-level locking — 30s default timeout, pid+timestamp stale-lock recovery (3x timeout), read-during-write returns pre-write state without lock
- [x] ac-04 [gate]: schema-version file present from day one, migration runner exists, idempotent (twice = no-op on second run)
- [ ] ac-05 [gate]: MCP server and CLI both call same Rust core — state-mutation parity (canonical files + state.db byte-identical), error format `<verb>: <category>: <sentence>`
- [ ] ac-06: Spec verbs — create/show/list/update/close/note; transactional spec.close updates linked cards' specs_array atomically; rejects if child tasks unfinished
- [ ] ac-07: Task verbs — ready/show/list/claim/update/done; append-only JSONL event stream; state reconstructs from last event; survives session reset
- [ ] ac-08: Memory verbs — remember/list/search; cross-session, cross-machine via git
- [ ] ac-09: Card verbs — show/list/search read-only; specs_array side-effect from spec.close is the only substrate-driven write
- [ ] ac-10: Choice verbs — show/list/search read-only
- [ ] ac-11: Session verb — prime; bounded output formula `f(N specs, M memories) ≤ 40 + 2*open_specs + min(M,10)`; K=10 memory cap
- [ ] ac-12 [gate]: Migration A (layout) — orbit/ → .orbit/, decisions/ → choices/, MD → YAML; worktree-validated; verbatim grep returns no matches; idempotent
- [ ] ac-13: Migration B (substrate) — bd issues/notes/memories → orbit-state files; full-coverage hash-set comparison (no spot-checking); .beads/ → .beads-archive/ gitignored
- [ ] ac-14: Six substantive skill rewrites — drive/implement/rally/review-spec/review-pr/audit; differentiated parity bar (state-mutating: byte-identical state; judgement-emitting: verdict + HIGH-finding equality); ALL six ship before dogfood opens
- [ ] ac-15: Path-only skill updates (8 skills) + CLAUDE.md bd→orbit replacement (deterministic grep) + README early-release notice verbatim
- [ ] ac-16 [gate]: CI round-trip gate — covers specs/cards/choices/memories/schema-version on every commit; tasks excluded (append-only); blocks merge on schema drift
- [ ] ac-17: CI orbit verify gate — index-rebuild verification on every commit; blocks merge on drift
- [ ] ac-18 [gate]: ≥1 week dogfood window in orbit repo — strict no-fallback to bd; restartable on verb breakage
- [ ] ac-19: v0.1.0 git tag pushed — internal-only (no GitHub Release); multi-machine binaries (mac + beelink); .beads-archive/ gitignored, .beads/ absent; README early-release notice present
- [ ] ac-20: .orbit/.gitignore ships with contracted ignore set (state.db/schema-version/locks/ ignored; cards/choices/specs/memories tracked); README install instructions for both platforms
- [ ] ac-21 [gate]: Week-1 cross-compile gate — skeleton binary builds on mac + beelink; MUST link SQLite (rusqlite); nm/otool verifies SQLite symbols
- [ ] ac-22 [gate]: Pre-dogfood atomic main-branch cutover — Migration A + B in single commit; .beads/ deletion + .gitignore for .beads-archive/ in same commit; no orbit-version skill runs against main pre-cutover; dogfood window timer starts at cutover commit

title: Spec 4: Rally skill — beads collapse
description: Rewrite /orb:rally so rally.yaml is removed and the bead dependency graph is durable state. Rally creates an epic bead at proposal approval; cards become children via promote.sh + bd update --parent <epic>. bd ready --type task --parent replaces TaskList. bd dep add wires disjointness ordering. Single-strike NO-GO becomes bd close --reason 'PARKED: ...'. Spec: orbit/specs/2026-05-01-rally-skill-beads-collapse/spec.yaml
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] ac-01 [gate]: Lock the input contract — goal+autonomy / epic-id / no-arg resume via bd list --type epic
- [x] ac-02 [gate]: Thin-card refusal preserved at proposal — ≥3 scenarios; no silent downgrade; modify-loop re-runs are pre-qualification retries
- [x] ac-03: Proposal stage produces epic + child beads — bd create -t epic + promote.sh per card + bd update --parent <epic>; no rally.yaml writes
- [x] ac-04: Decision-pack stage with snapshot-diff verification — sub-agent brief reads spec_dir from metadata; first violation re-briefs (retry), second parks via bd close --reason
- [x] ac-05: Consolidated decision gate + interview production — AskUserQuestion per card; updates rally_phase + rally_card_phase metadata
- [x] ac-06: Disjointness wiring — non-empty intersection encoded via bd dep add <later> <earlier>; supervised greenlight gated
- [x] ac-07: Implementation stage uses bd ready as queue — serial via bd ready --type task --parent <epic>; parallel via bd list + worktree + Agent run_in_background; sub-agents run /orb:drive <bead-id>
- [x] ac-08: Single-strike NO-GO via bd close --reason "PARKED: [<label>] <reason>" — six reason_label tokens preserved; no retry
- [x] ac-09: Mid-flight parallel→serial via single bd dep add invocation — no rally.yaml mutation
- [x] ac-10: PR strategy preserved — stacked + batched + parked-skip rule; no rally.yaml references
- [x] ac-11: Completion writes summary + sets rally_phase=complete + closes epic via bd close OR bd epic close-eligible; no archival step
- [x] ac-12: Resumption via bd list --type epic + rally_phase metadata; no file-presence detection; no drive.yaml resolution rule
- [x] ac-13 [gate]: Removal sweep — no rally.yaml / two-layer state / parked_constraint / TaskCreate / drive.yaml / file-presence / path-resolution / rally.yaml validation references
- [x] ac-14: Worked example — copy-pasteable command trace covering bd create -t epic, promote, parent, ready, dep add, close --reason
close_reason: rally complete: rewrote /orb:rally as a thin orchestrator over beads — rally.yaml removed, epic+child bead graph is durable state, bd dep add wires disjointness ordering, bd ready --type task --parent is the queue, single-strike park via bd close --reason 'PARKED: ...'. Decision-pack design + thin-card guard + 3-primitive verification preserved.

title: Spec 3: Drive skill — beads rewrite
description: Rewrite /orb:drive to use bead lifecycle. Promotion (promote.sh) replaces design→spec. Cold-fork review reads acceptance field instead of spec.yaml. Bead status replaces drive.yaml state machine. bd close --reason replaces drive completion flow. Agent promotes autonomously at drive start.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] ac-01 [gate]: Lock the input contract — card path / bead-id / no-arg resolution branches documented
- [x] ac-02 [gate]: Thin-card refusal preserved — full mode requires ≥3 scenarios; no silent downgrade
- [x] ac-03: Promote stage replaces design+spec — promote.sh card→bead + bd update --set-metadata for orchestration state
- [x] ac-04: Heartbeat (full only) — drive-checkin-<bead-id>, reads bead state via bd show + parse-acceptance.sh, self-terminates on bead.status closed
- [x] ac-05: Stage 1 review-spec — bead-snapshot + cycle-specific verdict path; canonical regex + synthetic-BLOCK constraint preserved byte-for-byte
- [x] ac-06: Stage 2 implement — delegates to /orb:implement <bead-id>; sets drive_stage on entry/exit; no inlined AC tracking
- [x] ac-07: Stage 3 review-pr — mirrors stage 1 with diff brief; severity dispatch + four-option verdict prompt preserved verbatim
- [x] ac-08: Completion — commit + PR + bd close --reason; drive_stage=complete; non-fatal heartbeat cleanup
- [x] ac-09: NO-GO — bd close --reason "NO-GO" + bd remember + promote new iteration bead with discovered-from edge + injected constraint history
- [x] ac-10: Escalation — structured summary (history from bd dep tree); one-shot ping; non-fatal heartbeat cleanup; drive_stage=escalated
- [x] ac-11: Resumption — drive_stage metadata is sole source; no drive.yaml read; no file-presence detection; heartbeat reconciliation re-runs
- [x] ac-12 [gate]: Removal sweep — no drive.yaml / interview.md / spec.yaml / spec-dir / drive-checkin-<spec-slug> / progress.md / file-presence references in SKILL.md
- [x] ac-13: Worked example — copy-pasteable card→bead→close trace
close_reason: drive completed: rewrote /orb:drive against beads — promote.sh collapses design+spec, drive_stage metadata replaces drive.yaml, iterations chain via discovered-from edges, cold-fork reads bead-snapshot.md, four-option verdict + synthetic-BLOCK + heartbeat preserved

title: Spec 2: Implement skill — beads rewrite
description: Rewrite /orb:implement to read ACs from bead acceptance field via parse-acceptance.sh instead of spec.yaml + progress.md. Track progress via bd update --acceptance. Gate enforcement via parse-acceptance.sh next-ac. Detours become sub-beads with discovered-from dependency. AC checking via parse-acceptance.sh check.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] ac-01 [gate]: Lock the input contract and active-bead resolution (bd list --status in_progress, single/zero/multiple branches)
- [x] ac-02: Pre-flight surface uses bd show + parse-acceptance.sh acs / next-ac; no progress.md write
- [x] ac-03: AC tracking and gate enforcement delegated to parse-acceptance.sh next-ac and check
- [x] ac-04: Detour escalation as sub-beads via bd create --parent + --deps "discovered-from:<parent>"
- [x] ac-05: Three-channel forward routing — sub-bead / top-level bead / memo
- [x] ac-06: Test-execution discipline preserved (Monitor + line-buffered grep + FIRST_FAILURE_NONINTERACTIVE_MARKER verbatim)
- [x] ac-07: Completion via parse-acceptance.sh has-unchecked + bd close --reason; NO-GO via bd close --reason "NO-GO: ..." + bd remember
- [x] ac-08 [gate]: Removal sweep — no progress.md / parse-progress.sh / TaskCreate / TaskUpdate / DRIFT_NOTICE / RESUME_REBUILD_WARNING / Spec hash / "## Detours" references in SKILL.md
- [x] ac-09: Worked example using orbit-6da.1 — copy-pasteable command trace
close_reason: Rewrote /orb:implement against beads: bead acceptance field is the single source of truth; parse-acceptance.sh next-ac/check drive the loop; detours escalate as sub-beads with discovered-from; progress.md, parse-progress.sh, TaskCreate/TaskUpdate, drift detection, resume reconcile, DRIFT_NOTICE, RESUME_REBUILD_WARNING removed; Monitor heuristic + FIRST_FAILURE_NONINTERACTIVE_MARKER preserved verbatim. Spec at orbit/specs/2026-05-01-implement-skill-beads-rewrite/.

title: Beads execution migration rally
description: Beads execution migration — rewrite implement, drive, and rally skills against beads
close_reason: rally complete: beads execution migration — three skill rewrites shipped (implement, drive, rally). Orbit's execution layer (drive.yaml, rally.yaml, progress.md, parse-progress.sh) collapsed into beads. Decision 0011-beads-execution-layer realised end-to-end.

value: 0-4-0-shipped: orbit 0.4.0 shipped 2026-05-01. Bead-native execution layer end-to-end. Subsumes orbit-6da.0 (foundation: bd-init, parse-acceptance.sh, promote.sh, PRIME.md, acceptance-field convention) + orbit-6da.1 (/orb:implement reads bead acceptance field as AC source of truth; progress.md / TaskCreate / drift detection removed; detours escalate as sub-beads with discovered-from edges) + orbit-6da.2 (/orb:drive collapses to promote→review-spec→implement→review-pr; state lives in bead metadata; iteration history via discovered-from graph) + orbit-6da.3 (/orb:rally onto bead dependency graph; rally.yaml removed; epic bead + child beads IS the rally) + orbit-6da.4 (card 0016: cold-fork reviewers read beads directly via bd show + parse-acceptance.sh; snapshot bridge removed; gate semantics propagate end-to-end via promote.sh; MADR 0013). Decisions: 0011 (substrate) + 0013 (cold-fork mapping); 0002 partially superseded for review-pr scope only. Migration playbook: MIGRATION-0.4.0.md. Card 0017 queued: /orb:setup is bead-aware.

agent-estimate-inflation-guard: Agent-authored effort estimates default to conservative-engineering quotes, not Claude-execution reality. Recut at Claude-pace before treating as planning input. Surfaced 2026-05-07 orbit-state v0.1 tabletop — initial estimate 33-43 days inflated; recut to 13-17 days. Pattern: divide mechanical-work estimates by ~3, keep wall-clock dogfood/window estimates as-is.

cron-firing-harness-gotchas: (1) /orb:schedule provisions Anthropic remote agents only — wrong tool for local cron contracts. (2) CronCreate durable:true is silently ignored on this harness — no .claude/scheduled_tasks.json written, dies with REPL. For unattended local scheduling use launchd (macOS) or systemd timers (Linux); see ops/decisions/0030. Always file the next-tabletop prompt as a sibling artefact at <project>/orbit/contracts/<date>-pass<N>-prep-prompt.md regardless of scheduler — the artefact is source of truth, scheduler is convenience. Surfaced 2026-05-03 in FineType pass-2 tabletop (Nightingale scribe).

history-rewrite-2026-05-01: orbit's git history was rewritten on 2026-05-01 via git filter-repo to scrub a private project name from blobs and commit messages, and to remove MIGRATION-0.4.0.md from history entirely (moved to private ops repo at playbooks/migration-0.4.0.md). All commit SHAs on main changed. 5 stale remote branches deleted; 10 stale local branches deleted; only main remains. Backup mirror at /tmp/orbit-backup-pre-rewrite-20260501-180000.git (auto-deletes ~2026-05-31). Residual leak: refs/pull/17/* on GitHub still serves the original commits via raw .diff/.patch URLs and gh API; web UI is clean. GitHub Support ticket would close this fully but was not filed — surface deemed limited. Anyone with a pre-2026-05-01 clone has divergent history; reset --hard origin/main is the recovery.

next-session-pilot-rollout: Card 0016 shipped in PR #17 (5de3773); 0.4.0 release blocker cleared. Next milestone is the pilot rollout per 0-4-0-rollout-plan: Arcform first (smallest blast radius), then FineType / Brightfield / Hydrofoil. Each project needs (a) bd 1.0.3+ verified at the same install path used here (/home/hugh/.local/bin/bd on Linux; verify Mac install path before kicking off Mac-side projects), (b) bd init in the project, (c) PRIME.md installed via bd-init.sh, (d) one trial drive against an existing card to validate the full bead-native flow under real workload. After Arcform validates, batch the remaining three.
orbit execution layer runs on beads — agents use bd ready, bd show, bd close instead of spec.yaml, progress.md, rally.yaml

tabletop-auq-hybrid-pattern: AskUserQuestion closes forks; prose opens them. Inside tabletop sessions, use prose for opening questions, scenario walks, and lateral naming (where amendments and creative reframes carry the value); use AUQ for closing picks, ambiguity resolution, halt-trigger confirmation, and option selection (where the value is 'which one'). Surfaced 2026-05-07 orbit-state v0.1 tabletop.
